Key Takeaways:
• Let’s go through a few tactical considerations for when you’re creating your
Prelaunch Content.
• When doing your emails, videos, sales letters, etc. make sure you aren’t dating it in
any way. Don’t mention dates, holidays, birthdays, seasons, etc.
• If shooting outside, you don’t want to show the seasons - make sure it’s a moderate
climate area. You have to be very careful with this…
• When doing emails and using autoresponders watch out for dates. Don’t mention
topical things like current events, the news, what’s going on right now, etc.
• Example: In one of my videos I did a fireside chat in June, and people started
analyzing whether the video was shot at a different time.
• It’s important to make sure your videos aren’t dated, because if people feel that
they are they’ll instantly “bounce” (leave) your video page.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
So now just a couple of quick tactical considerations. When you’re creating your
Prelaunch Content, including your emails, videos, the sales letter, sales video, anything
like that, one of the things you want to do is avoid dating it in any way.
So you don’t want to mention holidays, you don’t want to mention seasons, you
don’t want to mention the year, you want keep seasonality stuff out of there, don’t talk
about graduations, you probably even want to avoid birthdays. You don’t want to talk
about Christmas, you probably don’t want to shoot your video if it’s winter with all kinds
of snow in the background. If you’re going to shoot outside, try to make it as non-
seasonal as possible, you probably don’t want a lot of fall color in the trees and stuff. If
you live in Southern California or Florida, someplace where it’s always warm, you’re
probably safer that way.
You just want to always be thinking about whatever you’re saying and stuff in the
background, you want to keep anything that dates it out of there. Even in your product, if
you’re doing calls that are going to be recorded as part of the product you don’t want to
be mentioning dates in there and things like that.
Just as you go through all your stuff you definitely want to be careful about dates.
A lot of times in your sales letter you might want to mention “this shuts down on such
and such date” or “you’ve got to order by such and such date,” you’ve got to be real
careful with that.
Another thing is especially in your emails, if you’re going to try to go purely
evergreen and use autoresponders then you have to be real careful about how you handle
dates in there as well. Another good reason to possibly consider using broadcasts for
your emails instead.
If you’re evolving from you did a product launch and now you want to go
evergreen with the product launch, you definitely want to go back through all of your
content, all your prelaunch videos, blog posts, whatever you did with a fine tooth comb
and keep an eye out for any dates, keep an eye out for any holidays.
You don’t want to mention anything happening in the news, anything topical.
Those types of things, being topical, mentioning stuff in the news, that can be great in
launches to bring that into launches, but it will mess you up on Evergreen Launches. So,
just be real careful about all that stuff as you go through your Prelaunch Content.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
If you’re just creating your launch right now you might want to think ahead of
time and avoid mentioning any kind of stuff like that, any seasonality, any news, any
events, anything like that that could possibly date it.
I know in one of my videos I did a fireside chat and that launch was actually in
June when the fireside chat, I just wanted to do a fireside chat just to say I did a fireside
chat, and I had a nice fireplace, it made for a nice shot, but people were like, “Why are
you doing this in June? Was this an old video?” It actually wasn’t, but that just shows the
level that sometimes people get a little crazy about analyzing what you’re doing and they
wanted to know why I was sitting next to a fire in June. I guess that makes sense for
them to ask that, but I just wanted to do a fireside chat.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
